steps.title: Find the claim to illustrate
steps.content: The passage claims that general economic policy uncertainty may not fully reflect uncertainty in specific policy areas. The correct graph detail must show a clear mismatch between the general measure and a specific measure.

steps.title: Reject inaccurate graph claims
steps.content: Choice A claims a large 2009 difference between general and tax uncertainty, but the values are close. Choice B is false because general uncertainty is not lower than tax uncertainty every year. Choice C is false because tax uncertainty does not hit its low when general uncertainty peaks.

steps.title: Show the reversal
steps.content: The remaining choice shows divergence: general uncertainty is much lower than trade uncertainty in 2005 but much higher than trade uncertainty in 2010. The answer is D.
